Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

(5 comments)

I think this code is more difficult to understand than it needs to be, and this is going to continue to cause bugs in this area down the line.  It will also continue to get harder to read this code when we add the preprocessor directives to deprecate and then prohibit the usage of the old format.

I pushed my version of a fix for this bug here:

http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/18703/1/lustre/utils/lustre_cfg.c

Please take a look.  I think it simplifies the code a little.  I tried to compromise on allowing '=' in the values in the 'set_param param value' format by printing a warning message in that case.

commit.subject: LU-7796 lctl: set_param should report errors for wrong formats
commit.message:
LU-7796 lctl: set_param should report errors for wrong formats

"lctl set_param" should report error when there is no value following
the parameter, in such cases:
 - lctl set_param param
 - lctl set_param param=
 - mix of the above formats, e.g.
   "lctl set_param jobid_var= timeout=20 abde=efg debug"

Signed-off-by: Emoly Liu <emoly.liu@intel.com>
Change-Id: Ic7d75b38c26d2e36fd011c92737c8642a8d1612c
